description: RGB shows approximately 95% black no-data fill with only a narrow strip of dark-green vegetation visible at the very bottom edge; SWIR is similarly dominated by black no-data with a thin bright-green strip at the bottom.
labeler_notes: Large black no-data region makes the frame unusable for hazard assessment.

description: RGB shows heavy cloud coverage across nearly the entire frame with only fragmentary glimpses of dark-green forest beneath; SWIR confirms cloud with bright-white areas and partial lime-green patches where vegetation is visible through gaps.
labeler_notes: Cloud cover obscures the vast majority of the ground surface.

description: RGB is almost entirely black no-data with a thin bottom strip showing vegetation and cloud edge; SWIR matches — black no-data dominating with a narrow bright-green and faint-orange strip at the bottom edge.
labeler_notes: No-data fill covers well over 90% of the frame; insufficient ground coverage to assess.

description: RGB shows largely clear-sky dark-green forested mountainous terrain with visible road/track lines and a small cloud patch in the lower left; SWIR is uniformly bright lime-green indicating healthy intact vegetation, with only scattered minor red dots and a dark diagonal drainage line — no fresh landslide scar or bare-earth exposure is visible.
labeler_notes: Capture is on the event-peak date but imagery shows intact forest with no confirmed disturbance scar; scattered SWIR red dots are minor anomalies only.

description: RGB frame is nearly all black no-data, with only a very thin strip along the bottom edge showing green vegetation canopy and a small cloud fragment; SWIR similarly shows black no-data dominating, with a narrow strip of bright-green and minor yellow-green at the bottom.
labeler_notes: No-data fill dominates; insufficient ground visible for meaningful assessment.

description: RGB shows significant cloud cover across the lower half and center with dark-green intact forest visible in the upper portion; SWIR reveals healthy bright-green vegetation above the cloud line with cyan-toned patches in the lower-left suggesting exposed wet ground or moisture anomaly, but cloud coverage prevents confident identification of a landslide scar.
labeler_notes: Partial cloud limits ground assessment of the areas most likely to show landslide aftermath; cyan SWIR patches worth flagging but not conclusive.

description: RGB is almost entirely black no-data with only a tiny cloud-edged strip visible at the very bottom; SWIR shows the same near-total black no-data fill with a narrow light-green strip at the bottom edge.
labeler_notes: Frame dominated by no-data; effectively unusable.

description: RGB is fully covered by thick white cloud with no ground visible; SWIR shows a uniform cyan and light-teal pattern across the entire frame, consistent with an optically thick cloud layer blocking all surface signal.
labeler_notes: Complete cloud cover — no ground information available.

description: RGB shows thin haze or cloud veil over terrain with a mottled blue-grey texture limiting ground clarity; SWIR reveals a striking spatial divide — the left half is bright lime-green indicating healthy vegetation, while the right half is strongly dark with a dense branching network pattern consistent with heavily disturbed, cleared, or landslide-affected terrain.
labeler_notes: Capture is 7 days before the event peak; the sharp SWIR contrast between intact vegetation and the dark branching zone on the right is a significant anomaly warranting HIGH priority. RGB haze reduces confidence.

description: RGB displays a uniform blue-grey hazy/cloud texture throughout the frame suggesting thin cloud or aerosol veil over the terrain; SWIR shows mottled dark-green and purple tones across the full frame indicating either cloud-covered disturbed terrain or stressed vegetation, with no clearly localized hazard signature or bare-earth exposure visible.
labeler_notes: Haze and possible cloud cover reduce interpretation confidence across the whole frame; no discrete hazard feature identifiable.
